payload.query: How did Segal and Spaeth test this?

articles.title: Sea Levels Are Already Higher Than Many Scientists Think, New Study Shows
articles.abstract: Researchers found that a majority of studies on coastal sea levels underestimated how high water levels are, and hundreds of millions of people are closer to peril than previously thought.
articles.snippet: Researchers found that a majority of studies on coastal sea levels underestimated how high water levels are, and hundreds of millions of people are closer to peril than previously thought.
articles.byline: By Sachi Kitajima Mulkey and Mira Rojanasakul
keywords: Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas

articles.title: Sierra Nevada Avalanche Killed a Group of 6 Moms Who Bonded Over the Outdoors
articles.abstract: Families and friends of the women, many from the Bay Area, confirmed their identities. The women went on regular trips to the Tahoe region and were experienced skiers, the families said.
articles.snippet: Families and friends of the women, many from the Bay Area, confirmed their identities. The women went on regular trips to the Tahoe region and were experienced skiers, the families said.
articles.byline: By Sabrina Tavernise, Heather Knight and Soumya Karlamangla

articles.title: Ask a Journalist: Making a Compelling Argument
articles.abstract: Nicholas Kristof, an Opinion columnist, joins us in March to talk to teenagers all about opinion writing. Post your comments and questions by March 15.
articles.snippet: Nicholas Kristof, an Opinion columnist, joins us in March to talk to teenagers all about opinion writing. Post your comments and questions by March 15.

articles.title: ‘New Michelangelos’ and the ‘Dan Brown Approach’ to Art History
articles.abstract: Works newly attributed to the Renaissance artist had exciting stories behind them. But experts say they are unlikely to be by his hand.
articles.snippet: Works newly attributed to the Renaissance artist had exciting stories behind them. But experts say they are unlikely to be by his hand.

articles.title: Edward L. Deci, 83, Dies; Found Self-Determination as a Key to Happiness
articles.abstract: His work with his colleague Richard Ryan changed how psychologists understand human motivation and what people require to flourish.
articles.snippet: His work with his colleague Richard Ryan changed how psychologists understand human motivation and what people require to flourish.

articles.title: Jirdes Winther Baxter, 101, Dies; Last Survivor of Epidemic in Alaska
articles.abstract: An outbreak of diphtheria inspired a celebrated sled dog relay of nearly 700 miles to deliver lifesaving serum to the remote town of Nome.
articles.snippet: An outbreak of diphtheria inspired a celebrated sled dog relay of nearly 700 miles to deliver lifesaving serum to the remote town of Nome.

articles.title: Drew Struzan, Masterly Painter of Movie Posters, Dies at 78
articles.abstract: He created realistic imagery for the “Star Wars” franchise, the Indiana Jones films and the “Back to the Future” trilogy. He also put Alice Cooper in a tuxedo and tails for an album cover.
articles.snippet: He created realistic imagery for the “Star Wars” franchise, the Indiana Jones films and the “Back to the Future” trilogy. He also put Alice Cooper in a tuxedo and tails for an album cover.
keywords: Indiana Jones and the Kingdom of the Crystal Skull (Movie)

articles.title: Fred Segal Has a New Owner. Again.
articles.abstract: The storied Los Angeles retailer, once recognized as an arbiter of West Coast style, has been acquired by the mass-market brand Aritzia.
articles.snippet: The storied Los Angeles retailer, once recognized as an arbiter of West Coast style, has been acquired by the mass-market brand Aritzia.
keywords: Fred Segal Home (Los Angeles, Calif, Retailer)

articles.title: Experiment Shows Possibility of Martian Microbes Hitching a Ride to Earth
articles.abstract: Hardy bacteria in a lab survived pressures comparable to an asteroid strike on the red planet, suggesting a hypothetical scenario in which our planet was seeded with life.
articles.snippet: Hardy bacteria in a lab survived pressures comparable to an asteroid strike on the red planet, suggesting a hypothetical scenario in which our planet was seeded with life.

articles.title: Senator Helps Officers Forcibly Remove Protesting Veteran From Hearing
articles.abstract: The physical struggle, involving Senator Tim Sheehy of Montana, was captured on video. The protester, Brian McGinnis, said on social media that his arm was broken.
articles.snippet: The physical struggle, involving Senator Tim Sheehy of Montana, was captured on video. The protester, Brian McGinnis, said on social media that his arm was broken.
